title: KRS - Leadership Meeting
summary:
The meeting reviewed operational updates, a potential PNC-affiliated Medicare call-center client, and the KRS portal/app (My Insurance Coach) rollout and campaign plans. Mitch reported about 28 survey submissions and agreed to send one final reminder before the Typeform subscription ends. Matt described a near-final deal with a PNC-related group that has capital and aggressive hiring goals for a Medicare call center; no commitments were made beyond current system capabilities.

The team reviewed the portal MVP features and timeline — personalized profiles, localized perks, a dynamic top section, agent linkage, and planned GoiLevel integration for messaging and scheduling — and noted Kevin’s goal to complete a proof-of-concept before month-end and onboard agent Nadine as the initial pilot. Signup will use a Stripe checkout capturing name, email, and agent ID, but cannot include the proposed product checklist at point of sale. As an interim measure agents will manually enter client coverage data; Matt outlined a "turn in sheet" workflow that auto-generates an email and requires about two extra minutes of agent input, acknowledging limited support for frequent post-enrollment edits.

Rollout sequencing was set to trial with Nadine, then expand to Arizona if successful, aiming to align portal rollout with an autosync and app release by August 1st. The group agreed to vet 24/7 call-support vendors and clarified Ryan’s subaccount ownership and responsibilities for deleted workflows and lead routing. Finally, the team discussed a paid cross-sale campaign and whether to provide raw data or a managed, fee-based outreach service, favoring small pilots and revenue-share trials while deferring a universal policy and defining a limited set of standard reports.
